title: Total wealth by asset group
subtitle: This data is adjusted for differences in living costs between countries, and for inflation. It is measured in constant 2014 international-$.
caption:
Total wealth by asset group

This data is adjusted for differences in living costs between countries, and for inflation. It is measured in constant 2014 international-$.

This horizontal bar chart displays the total wealth by asset group for the world in 2014, measured in constant 2014 international dollars. The chart features four categories, with three positive values represented by blue bars extending to the right and one negative value represented by a thin line extending to the left. Human capital constitutes the largest asset group, with a value of $742.07 trillion, visually dominating the chart as the longest bar. Produced capital is the second-largest category, recorded at $303.55 trillion, which is less than half the length of the human capital bar. Natural capital follows with a value of $107.43 trillion, appearing as the shortest of the positive bars. At the bottom of the chart, net foreign assets are shown as a negative value of -$4.58 trillion. The visual arrangement clearly distinguishes the magnitude of human capital relative to produced and natural capital, while the negative value for net foreign assets is distinct in both direction and scale compared to the positive asset groups.
